title: Atalanthus resedifolius
intro: Atalanthus resedifolius, also known as the "resedegrass" or "sweet woodruff," is a perennial herbaceous plant in the family Gentianaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative effects. The bioactive constituents of Atalanthus resedifolius include volatile oils, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. In herbal medicine, Atalanthus resedifolius is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote relaxation, calm the nervous system, and support overall well-being.
